title: Podman in Openshift image pull issue
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman in OpenShift container does not pull images. 
I have installed podman on a OpenShift container and running as a root user. I am able to login to my private registry using podman. I am also able to pull hello-world image from docker registry. I am also able to pull some images from our private registry. But for other images, I get error.
Looks like podman has issue pulling images which run with non-root user.  

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. I am using Ubuntu 18.04 base image for my container

2. Install podman as per the instructions given on podman.io for ubuntu. (https://podman.io/getting-started/installation)

3. podman pull openshift/wildfly-101-cento7

**Describe the results you received:**
ERRO[0014] Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted 
  ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted
Trying to pull quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7...
  unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
Error: unable to pull openshift/wildfly-101-centos7: 2 errors occurred:
        * Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:45a2e645736c4c66ef34acce2407ded21f7a9b231199d3b92d6c9776df264729": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted
        * Error initializing source docker://quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7: unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successfully pull the image

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Issue happens for some images. I believe issue occurs for images which use user than other than root.

/etc/subuid and /etc/subgid  are empty.
root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# cat /etc/sub*id
root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp#

root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# cat /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces 
0
root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)

root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# whoami
root



**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:      2.0.4
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.4
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.18, commit: '
  cpus: 32
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: ucda-podman-2-n8b66
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 3.10.0-1127.el7.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 1530802176
  memTotal: 67364667392
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 2028h 32m 32.46s (Approximately 84.50 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: vfs
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus: {}
  imageStore:
    number: 2
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 1
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.14.4
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.0.4
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 2.0.4~1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Openshift 4.3.12 on IBM cloud.

comments.body:
If I try to pull this image from my laptop, I get a similar error
```
$ podman pull quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7
Trying to pull quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7...
  unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
Error: unable to pull quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7: Error initializing source docker://quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/openshift/wildfly-101-centos7: unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
```
I think you need to login to the registry in order to pull this image.

Even after login to quay.io repository, I am getting below error with podman. I am able to successfully pull the same image with docker.

root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# podman login quay.io
Authenticating with existing credentials...
Existing credentials are valid. Already logged in to quay.io

root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# podman pull centos/nginx-112-centos7
Trying to pull docker.io/centos/nginx-112-centos7:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob ab5ef0e58194 done  
Copying blob 41395b31da21 done  
Copying blob 4bb32ba4aeb6 done  
Copying blob 7464432c3f01 done  
Copying blob 0c1ab5ee9c31 done  
Copying blob b04f3cd8f5dc done  
Copying blob 0de1e67a7118 done  
Copying blob 209c879d58e3 done  
Copying config 23b35619ae done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
ERRO[0004] Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted 
  ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted
Trying to pull quay.io/centos/nginx-112-centos7:latest...
  unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
Error: unable to pull centos/nginx-112-centos7:latest: 2 errors occurred:
        * Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:ab5ef0e5819490abe86106fd9f4381123e37a03e80e650be39f7938d30ecb530": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted
        * Error initializing source docker://quay.io/centos/nginx-112-centos7:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/centos/nginx-112-centos7: unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized



Successfully pulling image with Docker cli -
docker pull centos/nginx-112-centos7
Using default tag: latest
latest: Pulling from centos/nginx-112-centos7
ab5ef0e58194: Already exists                                                                                                                                                                                       b04f3cd8f5dc: Pull complete                                                                                                                                                                                        4bb32ba4aeb6: Pull complete                                                                                                                                                                                        0c1ab5ee9c31: Pull complete                                                                                                                                                                                        41395b31da21: Pull complete                                                                                                                                                                                        7464432c3f01: Pull complete                                                                                                                                                                                        0de1e67a7118: Pull complete                                                                                                                                                                                        209c879d58e3: Pull complete                                                                                                                                                                                        Digest: sha256:af171c38298e64664a9f999194480ce7e392858e773904df22f7585a1731ad0d
Status: Downloaded newer image for centos/nginx-112-centos7:latest
docker.io/centos/nginx-112-centos7:latest

Trying to pull image from our private registry -

root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# podman login us.icr.io
Authenticating with existing credentials...
Existing credentials are valid. Already logged in to us.icr.io
root@ucda-podman-2-n8b66:/tmp# podman pull us.icr.io/shrini-poc/centos7-tools
Trying to pull us.icr.io/shrini-poc/centos7-tools...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 6010c765ce31 done  
Copying blob ba2d8e4c4afd done  
Copying blob 524b0c1e57f8 done  
Copying blob 6107fd21e8d1 done  
Copying blob d2058699b909 done  
Copying blob fe3f1747be75 done  
Copying blob e8d8c950b98f done  
Copying config 3220b61338 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
ERRO[0009] Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted 
  ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted
Error: unable to pull us.icr.io/shrini-poc/centos7-tools: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:524b0c1e57f8ee5fee01a1decba2f301c324a6513ca3551021264e3aa7341ebc": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: operation not permitted

are you using overlay?

You may need to switch to vfs, or use fuse-overlayfs

Ubuntu supports rootless overlay, but that is not part of the vanilla kernel and it is not supported by Podman
Any solution or suggestion on this issue ?

We really have no idea.
You are running as root and with vfs storage and getting permission denied while trying to commit.

Of course you are using podman 2.0 on Centos 7, which is not something we really support.  Any chance you could try this on Centos 8 or RHEL8?

Ok, I just went back and read the beginning, you are running podman within a container, on a Centos 7 host?

Is the container fully privileged?

Yes the container is fully privileged. I am using privileged security context. 
My container base image is Ubuntu 18.04
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
os: linux
Is podman being run as non root inside of the OpenShiftContainer or is it running as root?
Since we have had no answer, I am closing for lack of information, reopen if new information arises.
